title: Multimodal Maple Plant Disease Detection Using EfficientNet and Transformer-Based Semantic Fusion
abstract: This paper presents a novel multimodal deep learning framework for maple plant disease detection by integrating visual and semantic information. Traditional systems rely primarily on features extracted from leaf images, which often leads to misclassification in cases of visually similar symptoms. To address this limitation, the proposed approach combines EfficientNet-based convolutional neural networks feature extraction with transformerbased language models, including BERT FLAN-T5, encoding. A Multilayer Perceptron (MLP)-based fusion mechanism is employed integrate textual features, enabling effective cross-modal learning. The model evaluated balanced dataset 2,000 images associated descriptions. Experimental results demonstrate that achieves an accuracy 94.8%, outperforming vision-only text-only models significant margin. Ablation studies comparative analysis confirm effectiveness provides robust scalable solution intelligent has potential applications smart agriculture systems.

title: China’s marine carbon sink capacity assessment and potential projection: a machine learning approach
abstract: The intensification of global climate change poses severe challenges to ecosystems and human development. Marine carbon sinks, as a critical natural solution, have placed their potential assessment trend prediction at the centre governance policymaking. As world’s largest emitter, China urgently requires scientifically grounded identification incremental regulatory pathways marine sinks achieve its “Dual Carbon” goals. This study employs panel data from 11 coastal provinces municipalities in mainland China, specifically Liaoning, Hebei, Tianjin, Shandong, Jiangsu, Shanghai, Zhejiang, Fujian, Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan (2005–2022) integrates multidimensional indicators spanning environmental conditions, activities, policy measures. In this predictive framework that combines machine learning with interpretability tools was also developed. Using XGBoost capture complex nonlinear relationships, model achieves accuracy 95.7%, SHAP analysis applied quantify marginal contributions threshold effects key drivers. Key findings include following: (1) number reserves, mariculture areas, total wastewater discharge are identified core drivers, while chlorophyll-a concentration research personnel serve important moderators—each exhibiting distinct “ecological thresholds”. (2) Multi-scenario projections for 2023–2032 indicate Green Development scenario yields highest annual sink (4.0061 million tC), surpassing Business-As-Usual (3.2133 tC) Economy-Priority (3.0872 scenarios. latter shows an initial decline 13.4% due deviation ecological thresholds. (3) Significant regional heterogeneity is observed: Northern Coastal Economic Belt dominated by mariculture, EP ≈ BAU &gt; GP; Eastern primarily driven urbanisation rate. With GP substantially outperforming others, Southern follows dual-core-driven pattern sea surface temperature, where demonstrates both optimal stable outcomes. provides scalable, data-driven approach projecting dynamics, offering actionable insights adapting management evidence-based formulation other maritime regions.

abstract: Biophysical elements of forests are essential for tree health, biodiversity, and ecosystem services, with their effects increasingly influenced by climate change global environmental dynamics.
